title: Seeds, Origins, Paths, Query Space, Budgets, and Stop Policy · Program Brief: Stop And Contact Policy
summary: A seed expands to subdomains, downloads, calendars, search results, and logout links while the crawler has no page, byte, time, or incident limit. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the stop and contact policy increment in bounded crawl-scope policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, c…
objectives: Define automatic stop signals, manual kill paths, owner contact, partial-output handling, cleanup, and restart authorization.
Budget pages, depth, requests, redirects, bytes, decompressed bytes, time, concurrency, retries, disk, browser contexts, and report rows.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-stop-and-contact-policy — Define automatic stop signals, manual kill paths, owner contact, partial-output handling, cleanup, and restart authorization.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-crawl-scope-policy-4
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-crawl-scope-policy-4.
# Case 1655: a local-server failure rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def worked_scraper_crawl_scope_policy_4(admitted_origins: int = 1, admitted_paths: int = 8, page_budget: int = 240, byte_budget: int = 12000000, stop_paths: int = 5) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "authorizationworked4",
        "url1655",
        "response4dekrow",
        "treeworked41655",
        "frontier1655worked4",
        "report4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if admitted_origins != 1:
        return False, admitted_origins, "origin-scope"
    if admitted_paths < 1:
        return False, admitted_paths, "path-policy"
    if page_budget < 1 or byte_budget < 1024:
        return False, page_budget, "resource-budget"
    if stop_paths < 3:
        return False, stop_paths, "stop-policy"
    assert admitted_origins == 1 and stop_paths >= 3
    return True, page_budget, "bounded-crawl-scope"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Build Stop And Contact Policy evidence · Theory Stop And Contact Pol…
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-stop-and-contact-policy-11rpas" that uses case 5005 to validate seed identity, origin and path admission, resource budgets, and automatic and manual stop policy. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-stop-and-contact-policy-11rpas
# Competency: scraper-stop-and-contact-policy.
# Case 5005: a privacy-minimized internal crawl.
# Operating constraint: no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def evidence_scraper_stop_and_contact_policy_11rpas(admitted_origins: int = 1, admitted_paths: int = 8, page_budget: int = 240, byte_budget: int = 12000000, stop_paths: int = 5) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_11rpas = "11rpas-sapr11-5005"
    assert evidence_variant_11rpas
    evidence_axes_11rpas = (
        "authorization11rpas",
        "url5005",
        "responsesapr11",
        "tree11rpas5005",
        "frontier500511rpas",
        "reportsapr115005",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_11rpas)) == 6
    if admitted_origins != 1:
        return False, admitted_origins, "origin-scope"
    if admitted_paths < 1:
        return False, admitted_paths, "path-policy"
    if page_budget < 1 or byte_budget < 1024:
        return False, page_budget, "resource-budget"
    if stop_paths < 3:
        return False, stop_paths, "stop-policy"
    assert admitted_origins == 1 and stop_paths >= 3
    return True, page_budget, "bounded-crawl-scope"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Stop And Contact Policy structural check is only one gate. bounded crawl-scope policy must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For Authorized crawling and Python Build Stop And Contact Policy evidence · Theory Stop And Contact Pol…, name the owner, authorization, stakeholder, revision, runtime, dependency, seed, URL, origin, robots, response, parser, selector, record, frontier, privacy, report, stop, recovery, and transfer boundary, Stop And Contact Policy precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized crawling and Python Build Stop And Contact Policy evidence · Theory Stop And Contact Pol…, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, URL admission, robots, fetch, redirect, decode, parse, extract, provenance, schedule, report, rejection, stop, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory site-quality team handles case 3838 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate seed identity, origin and path admission, resource budgets, and automatic and manual stop policy; no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define automatic stop signals, manual kill paths, owner contact, partial-output handling, cleanup, and restart authorization.

For Authorized crawling and Python Build Stop And Contact Policy evidence · Theory Stop And Contact Pol…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-authorization, URL, robots rule, response, encoding, markup, selector, frontier, timing, session, browser, privacy, security, export, failure, recovery, and controlled owner transfer evidence: A crawler should keep retrying until it eventually completes.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Stop And Contact Policy model · Theory Stop And Contact P…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Stop And Contact Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 8695 during a privacy-minimized internal crawl. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate seed identity, origin and path admission, resource budgets, and automatic and manual stop policy; personal data must remain minimized and redacted; then change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define automatic stop signals, manual kill paths, owner contact, partial-output handling, cleanup, and restart authorization.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Stop And Contact Policy model · Theory Stop And Contact P….
steps.why: Retrieving Stop And Contact Policy through the concrete bounded crawl-scope policy case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Stop And Contact Policy model · Theory Stop And Contact P…, keep Stop And Contact Policy connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define automatic stop signals, manual kill paths, owner contact, partial-output handling, cleanup, and restart authorization.
